summary.intent: Implement Phase 1 of the External CLI Agent Bridge for Moltis — foundation crate, core types/traits, config schema, session metadata, and service-traits wiring so CLI agents (Claude Code, opencode, Codex, Pi, ACP) can be bound to chat sessions.
summary.outcome: Created the `crates/external-agents/` crate with all core types, traits, bridge delta-sync logic, feature-gated runtime stubs, and wired it into the workspace, config schema, session metadata, service-traits bundle, and downstream consumers. All 9 unit tests pass, full workspace compiles clean, clippy and fmt pass.

repo: Feature flags default to on (opt-out, not opt-in) per CLAUDE.md policy — all runtime features in external-agents are default-enabled.

The `Services` bundle in `crates/service-traits/src/lib.rs` must be updated alongside any new service trait; downstream consumers (`crates/gateway/src/services.rs`, `crates/graphql/tests/integration.rs`) construct `Services {}` literals and must be patched when new fields are added.
`crates/graphql/tests/integration.rs` has a mock `Services` struct construction that must be kept in sync with the real `Services` struct.
Workspace deps use `portable-pty` already (consumed by `crates/web/`) — safe to add as dependency in new crates without adding a new external dep.
`taplo fmt` is used for TOML formatting and must be run after any Cargo.toml changes.
The nightly toolchain `nightly-2025-11-30` is pinned for `rustfmt`.
Beads (internal issue tracker) issues must be created, claimed, and closed as part of the PR workflow.

code.finding: SessionEntry has struct literal constructors in multiple places (chat/src/lib.rs test helpers) that must be updated when new fields are added; new fields need `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = 'Option::is_none')]` to stay backwards-compatible with existing SQLite records.
Contains test-only SessionEntry struct literal construction that must be updated when SessionEntry gains new fields.
Uses `to_services_with_chat()` which builds a `Services` literal — must be updated for each new service added to the bundle.
Clippy flagged a redundant closure in bridge.rs that needed fixing before the crate was clean.

workflow: Parallel tool calls (Read, Write, Edit) significantly speed up crate scaffolding when files are independent.
Running `cargo check -p <new-crate>` before `cargo check --workspace` lets you isolate new-crate errors from downstream breakage.
Format and clippy should be run on the new crate before running workspace-wide checks to keep signal clean.
Struct literal construction sites are a common source of compile breaks when adding fields — grep for `StructName {` early.

friction: Multiple iterative reads of the same large files (Cargo.toml, schema.rs, validate.rs, service-traits lib.rs) due to needing different line ranges — required many sequential Read calls to piece together complete file content.
SessionEntry struct literals scattered across chat/lib.rs test helpers and sessions/metadata.rs caused compile breaks that required separate discovery and fix steps.
Services struct construction in gateway/src/services.rs and graphql integration tests both broke silently until compile — no single authoritative list of all construction sites.
taplo fmt needed a separate pass after all Rust edits were done, adding an extra step at the end.
open_items: Phase 2: SessionBridge implementation with actual delta sync + compaction reconciliation persisted to SQLite KV (BridgeState).
Phase 3: PtyBackend and TmuxBackend implementations reusing crates/web/src/terminal.rs patterns.
Phase 4: ClaudeCodeRuntime with -p mode, interactive tmux mode, --resume/--continue reattachment.
Phase 5: Gateway integration — routing check in LiveChatService::send(), send_via_external_agent(), external_agent.* RPC methods, GonData addition.
Phase 6: Web UI — session binding cards, header badge, terminal tab integration.
Phase 7: Codex CLI (JSON-RPC), ACP generic runtime, opencode (tmux), Pi agent (JSON-RPC) — all runtime stubs exist but are unimplemented.
Phase 8: Channel integration for externally-bound sessions.
Runtime stubs (claude_code.rs, opencode.rs, codex.rs, pi_agent.rs, acp.rs) are scaffold-only with `todo!()` bodies — no actual PTY/tmux/JSON-RPC logic yet.
PromptDeliveryMode variants exist in types.rs but prompt.rs module referenced in the plan was not created — delivery logic is deferred to Phase 3/4.
pty_backend.rs and tmux_backend.rs modules referenced in the plan architecture were not created in Phase 1 — deferred to Phase 3.
